summary: Attempted to add a C match for fighter collision function func_8007B5AC in ftcoll.c plus a header prototype in ftcoll.h, but the PR was closed unmerged and explicitly marked as superseded by #576. The proposed implementation initialized ftHurt fields from an s32 data array, resolved a hurt bone JObj via Fighter.x5E8_fighterBones and x40_bone_index, byte-copied Vec3-like float data through s32 pointers to avoid floating-point register/double behavior, set x1C_scale, and set Fighter.x221A_flag.bits.b6. Review focused less on the local C body and more on decomp build/linking mechanics: removing a function from the middle of ftcoll.s breaks object ordering and matching unless the whole asm unit is handled correctly or both C and asm are wired through obj_files.mk.

key_files.role: Added proposed C implementation of func_8007B5AC and helper inline_fn.
Discussed in review because deleting func_8007B5AC from the middle of the asm file would break link order/matching; final diff excerpt no longer shows asm deletion.
Mentioned by review as needing both ftcoll.c and ftcoll.s entries if C and asm are both meant to build.

what_changed: Added func_8007B5AC(Fighter* arg0, ftHurt* arg1, s32* arg2) to src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c.
Added an inline helper inline_fn returning logical negation of a BOOL argument; reviewer suggested using BOOL rather than int/u32 for the helper signature.
func_8007B5AC assigned ftHurt fields x40_bone_index, x44_hurt_kind, x48_is_grabbable, x0_bone_state, x20_jobj, x4_hurt1_offset data, and x1C_scale from arg2 and Fighter bone data.
The implementation used integer loads/stores and s32 pointer casts to copy Vec3-like float bytes into x4_hurt1_offset instead of producing floating-point loads.
Added a prototype in src/melee/ft/ftcoll.h: void func_8007B5AC(Fighter *arg0, ftHurt *arg1, s32 *arg2);

Initial attempts apparently removed func_8007B5AC from asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s, but reviewers warned this could not match because function order matters when linking; author restored the asm according to comments.
PR was closed unmerged after a comment saying it was superseded by #576.
smart_moves: The C body attempted to preserve exact codegen by treating the Vec3-like float data as raw s32 words, avoiding GFPR/floating-point behavior that could arise from normal f32 copies.
The author reran clang-format after feedback and fixed indentation issues caused or masked by a missing header semicolon.
The author restored the asm after review explained that removing a function from the middle of an asm file is not safe for matching.
decomp_lessons: Do not remove a single function from the middle of an asm file and replace it with C unless the surrounding object/link arrangement preserves function order; order matters when linking and can make the result fail to match.

If matching one function inside a larger asm translation unit, either leave the asm in place and keep the C as reference/example until surrounding functions are matched, or port the relevant asm into the C file as inline asm and match the function in place.
When both C and asm versions are involved, obj_files.mk must be configured so the intended objects are built; otherwise the build may silently compile the asm and ignore the new C.
Header prototypes must include trailing semicolons; a missing semicolon can confuse formatting and syntax downstream.
For matching raw data movement into float fields, integer/pointer-cast byte copies may be necessary to avoid unwanted floating-point register usage.
A decomp.me/permuter result may need cleanup before review, including removal of comments like 'Permuter spit this out' and replacement of register-style variable names when possible.
naming_conventions: Existing unknown function naming retained address-style symbol func_8007B5AC.
The prototype used project-style primitive and struct types: Fighter*, ftHurt*, s32*.
Reviewer preferred BOOL for boolean helper return/argument types instead of int/u32 in inline_fn.
Struct fields remained offset-based names such as x40_bone_index, x44_hurt_kind, x48_is_grabbable, x0_bone_state, x20_jobj, x4_hurt1_offset, x1C_scale, x5E8_fighterBones, and x221A_flag.bits.b6.
Temporary names like R0, R7, new_var, p, and temp were present in the proposed match and appear permuter/register-driven rather than semantically finalized.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used raw s32 copies into x4_hurt1_offset to copy adjacent Vec3-like f32 fields without loading them as floating-point values.
Computed the Fighter bone entry offset as (x40_bone_index << 4) & 0xfffffff0 and loaded the JObj pointer from Fighter.x5E8_fighterBones plus that offset.
Included an empty if condition using inline_fn(R7) repeatedly, likely as a codegen artifact from the matching attempt; evidence does not clarify its semantic purpose.
Review emphasized link-order preservation: deleting func_8007B5AC from the middle of ftcoll.s will not match because later symbols shift.
Recommended alternatives were to keep the asm function and treat the C as an example, or convert the entire ftcoll.s into inline asm in ftcoll.c and match func_8007B5AC in place.
Review noted that if C and asm are both present, ftcoll.c and ftcoll.s must both be added to obj_files.mk or the new C may be ignored.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested changing the helper to inline BOOL inline_fn(BOOL arg0).

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested removing the comment 'Permuter spit this out'.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Warned that order matters when linking, so a function cannot be removed from the middle of ftcoll.s without breaking the match.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested either leaving the asm and using the C as an example until the rest is matched, or converting the entire ftcoll.s to inline asm in ftcoll.c and matching func_8007B5AC in place.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Pointed out the ftcoll.h prototype needed a trailing semicolon.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Noted indentation should be four spaces and suspected clang-format may have failed due to the missing semicolon.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Recommended rerunning clang-format after fixing syntax.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Explained that the build was currently compiling asm and ignoring the C; both ftcoll.c and ftcoll.s needed obj_files.mk entries, potentially requiring asm to be ported into C as-is.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Stated that the PR was superseded by #576.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #576 to see the superseding accepted solution for func_8007B5AC.
Search current ftcoll.c and ftcoll.s handling for examples where asm functions were ported into C with inline asm while preserving link order.
Check obj_files.mk conventions for building mixed C and asm translation units in the fighter subsystem.
Look for later renames or struct improvements around ftHurt fields x4_hurt1_offset, x1C_scale, and Fighter.x5E8_fighterBones.
